name: rad-planner
description:
Plan a project before you write code — or replan after a pivot, deepen an existing plan, or check for drift between plan and reality. rad-planner walks you through a five-phase planning conversation (Constitution → Scope → Sequence → Quality Gates → Doc-Set Recommendation), then writes the approved doc set as the plan's first executed milestone. The plan is the deliverable; the docs follow it.

Four entry points cover the lifecycle:
- `--full` — greenfield plan from scratch
- `--improve` — deepen or continue an existing plan
- `--drift` — compare reality to the plan
- `--pivot` — substantial re-plan with disposition manifest

A read-only `--assessment` mode answers "where does this project stand?" without writing anything. `--auto` produces DRAFT-labeled strawmen for unattended runs and does not write ADRs (candidate decisions land in `docs/planning/proposed-decisions.md` for review).

Two project-story skills (`/project-story`, `/refresh-story`) turn the canonical doc set into plain-English narrative for non-developers, collaborators, or your future self. Five mechanical validators back the workflow: `plan-lint.py`, `status-validator.py`, `doc-redundancy.py`, `doc-contradiction.py`, plus `assess-planning-velocity.py` (NEW in v4.6: overplanning signals).

**v4.8 — compact milestone contract embedded in `current.md`.** M6 now writes a `### Session contract` sub-section near the top of each milestone's active section, containing the 7-field build-readiness gate block: Current milestone, Goal, In scope, Out of scope, Files likely touched, Acceptance criteria, Stop and ask if. Derived from canonical sections (Objective / Acceptance criteria / Planned changes / Stop conditions / vision non-goals) — not authored separately. `plan-lint.py` validates all 7 fields are present with non-placeholder values. The contract is the render target rad-session 5.5+ surfaces at `/startup` as the first user-visible block, putting the build-readiness gate literally in the session's context window before the first tool call.

**v4.7 — Lanes section in the operating manual.** `/plan` M6 now writes a verbatim **Lanes** section into `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` covering role separation: what the human decides, what the agent may do during planning, what the agent may do during coding, what the agent must NOT do. Agent-agnostic template. Owned by rad-planner per the sectioned-writer rule; rad-session 5.4+ recognizes the section as Constitution-band and never modifies it. Idempotent — existing user edits to the section are preserved on re-run.

**v4.6 — build-readiness gate alignment.** Three additive changes close gaps against the planning-discipline framework: (1) `docs/planning/current.md` schema gains two required sections — **Guardrails** (what the agent must NOT change while executing this milestone) and **User-visible behavior** (what an end user can observe after this milestone ships). `plan-lint.py` enforces both. (2) `docs/planning/parked.md` is now first-class — M2 conversation offers parking as a third option alongside in-scope and non-goal for ambiguous ideas that aren't worth foreclosing. (3) `/plan --assessment` surfaces planning-velocity signals (edits-since-last-code-commit, AC-rewrite-count, planning-doc-growth-ratio, time-since-last-code-commit) to catch the planning-as-avoidance pattern. Per-project threshold overrides via CLI flags.

Aligned with published OpenAI/Anthropic project-structure research: three-band memory model (operating manual / project memory / execution state), intent-vs-reality split between `planning/current.md` and `status.md`, CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md as conditional operating manuals based on agent scope. Pairs with rad-session for the full lifecycle; works standalone.
